AI/ML Research

Venkat Chavan N

AI/ML Researcher | Neuro-Symbolic AI, LLM Evaluation & Multi-Agent Learning

I am an AI/ML researcher with an MSc in Big Data and Artificial Intelligence from SRH University of Applied Sciences, Berlin. My work focuses on building and evaluating AI systems that move beyond surface-level pattern matching toward structured reasoning, retrieval-grounded generation, and safety-aware decision-making.

My research spans neuro-symbolic AI, LLM evaluation, hierarchical retrieval-augmented generation, multi-agent reinforcement learning for autonomous driving, Sanskrit NLP, and privacy-preserving AI systems.

Publications Research Projects Download CV GitHub LinkedIn
Berlin, Germany: open to relocation venkat.chavan.n@gmail.com Google Scholar

Research Interests

Active research clusters across reasoning, evaluation, and trustworthy AI.

Trustworthy LLM Systems

Hallucination control, evidence grounding, RAG evaluation, citation accuracy, and adversarial robustness of retrieval-augmented language models.

Neuro-Symbolic AI & Structured Reasoning

Knowledge graphs, symbolic constraints, rule-based reasoning, and structured representations as a route to reasoning beyond surface-level pattern matching.

Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning

Coordinated autonomous driving in mixed-traffic environments, zero-shot transfer, safety-rule evaluation, and GNN/GAT-enhanced agent coordination.

Language Model Evaluation

SanskritEval, sandhi segmentation, morphological generalisation, low-resource NLP, and benchmark design that separates linguistic structure from surface patterns.

Privacy-Preserving & Security-Aware AI

Encrypted vector operations, privacy-preserving retrieval, cyber threat intelligence, defensive AI tooling, and PII-aware evidence handling.

Publications & Scholarly Work

Peer-reviewed publications, theses, and manuscripts in preparation. Status is conservative; only externally verified work is marked as published.

Research Projects

Research-aligned projects across reasoning, evaluation, multi-agent learning, and trustworthy AI. Engineering and creative side projects are listed under Engg-Projects.

H-RAG for Cyber Threat Intelligence

RAGKnowledge GraphsCTILLM EvaluationHallucination Control

A hierarchical retrieval-augmented generation system for cyber threat intelligence analysis, designed to preserve document structure across executive summaries, technical details, and atomic evidence units.

Methods: FAISS IVF, Sentence-Transformers, hierarchical chunking, lightweight CTI knowledge graph, local LLM inference, citation-grounded generation.

Evaluation: Internal evaluation across factual accuracy, citation accuracy, retrieval performance, hallucination rate, and latency.

Status: Accepted at international conference, 2026

Details Code

Coordinated Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Autonomous Driving

MARLAutonomous DrivingGNNsSafetyZero-shot Transfer

MSc thesis research on coordinated multi-agent reinforcement learning in mixed-traffic autonomous driving environments. Defended with grade 1.0/4.0 (S-tier).

Methods: MADDPG / MADDPG++, GAT-based coordination, CARLA mixed-traffic scenarios, safety-rule evaluators.

Evaluation: Thesis grade 1.0/4.0; internal evaluation under safety rules and zero-shot transfer scenarios.

Status: Completed: Thesis grade 1.0/4.0 (S-tier)

Details

SanskritEval: A Benchmark for Linguistic Generalisation in Sanskrit NLP

LM EvaluationLow-resource NLPMorphologyBenchmark Design

Benchmark suite for evaluating language models on Sanskrit sandhi segmentation and morphological case agreement using mBERT, XLM-R, IndicBERT, and MuRIL.

Methods: Sandhi segmentation, morphological acceptability minimal pairs, transformer fine-tuning, rule-based baselines.

Evaluation: Rule-based baseline reaches 1.000 F1; transformer models evaluated under controlled splits.

Status: Completed

Details Code

Onco-Variant-Guard: Neuro-Symbolic Cancer Variant Classification

Neuro-Symbolic AIBiomedical NLPKnowledge GraphsHallucination Control

Neuro-symbolic biomedical AI system for cancer variant classification combining evidence-grounded LLM explanations, an automatically extracted rule base, and a knowledge graph.

Methods: TF-IDF baseline, automatic rule extraction, knowledge-graph reasoning, RAG, evidence-only generation.

Evaluation: Baseline classifier at 59.3% accuracy; multi-layer hallucination prevention with knowledge-graph validation.

Status: Completed

Details Code

ObserveML: Observability Framework for LLM Applications

LLM EvaluationObservabilityReliabilityRAG Quality

Observability layer for LLM applications: latency, token cost, hallucination markers, RAG retrieval quality, and semantic drift, surfaced through a real-time dashboard.

Methods: ClickHouse trace storage, semantic-drift monitoring, hallucination confidence scoring, multi-language SDKs.

Evaluation: Engineering benchmarks for SDK overhead and trace-query throughput.

Status: Completed

Details Code

Procurement Anomaly Detection

Anomaly DetectionExplainabilityCivic AIPublic Sector

Applied ML system for anomaly detection in public procurement data, combining unsupervised methods with explainability for civic transparency.

Methods: Isolation Forest, LOF, SHAP, rule-based auditing, dbt transformations.

Evaluation: Empirical evaluation on Finnish procurement data with audit-aligned thresholds.

Status: Completed

Details Code

safe-evidence-redactor: Privacy-First Redaction Library and MCP Server

PrivacyDefensive AIPII RedactionMCP

Defensive AI tooling for offline PII redaction in security reports, logs, and AI outputs, exposed as a library, CLI, and MCP server.

Methods: Pattern-based redaction with category allowlists, JSON path tracking, MCP integration.

Evaluation: Internal pattern-coverage benchmarks; integrated and validated by phish-triage-mcp.

Status: Released: v0.1.2

Details Code

safe-web-audit-mcp: Consent-Based Defensive Web Audit

Defensive AIMCPConsent-based AuditingSecurity

Defensive Model Context Protocol server for consent-based, non-invasive website security audits.

Methods: MCP tooling, consent verification, SSRF guards, defensive audit checks.

Evaluation: Released open-source; consent and SSRF guards validated against test scenarios.

Status: Released: v0.2.1

Details Code

phish-triage-mcp: Local-First Phishing Triage MCP Server

Defensive AIMCPPhishing TriagePrivacy

Local-first MCP server that performs static analysis of suspicious emails, SMS, and URLs without contacting external services.

Methods: Static header parsing, lookalike-domain detection, PII redaction, MCP tooling.

Evaluation: Released open-source with unit-tested detection rules.

Status: Released: v0.2.0

Details Code

mcp-consent-ledger: Authorisation Proof Library for Defensive MCP Servers

Defensive AIMCPConsentAuthorisation

Reusable consent and authorisation-proof library for defensive MCP servers, with an auditable, local-only ledger.

Methods: SHA-256 token hashing, DNS TXT and well-known proof, SSRF-guarded fetcher.

Evaluation: Released library; verification logic validated against multi-string TXT records.

Status: Released: v0.1.1

Details Code

MV-Coach-Eval: HAR Benchmarking Platform

EvaluationHARCalibrationUncertainty

Benchmarking platform for Human Activity Recognition with calibration, robustness testing, LOSO generalisation, and Monte Carlo Dropout uncertainty.

Methods: ECE, LOSO cross-validation, Monte Carlo Dropout, robustness testing.

Evaluation: Internal benchmark coverage with Hydra-based reproducible runs.

Status: Completed

Details Code

Research Agenda

Long-term research direction and motivation.

My long-term research goal is to develop AI systems that are not only accurate, but also interpretable, evidence-grounded, and robust under distribution shift. Across my work, I am interested in the gap between pattern recognition and reasoning: when does a model truly learn structure, and when is it exploiting shortcuts in data?

My recent projects approach this question from multiple directions: hierarchical RAG for cyber threat intelligence, benchmark design for Sanskrit linguistic generalisation, neuro-symbolic biomedical reasoning, and coordinated multi-agent learning for autonomous driving. A common theme across these projects is evaluation: building systems whose outputs can be traced, tested, compared, and challenged.

I am particularly interested in PhD and research roles involving trustworthy LLMs, neuro-symbolic AI, retrieval-augmented generation, multi-agent learning, causal representation learning, and AI evaluation methodology.

Research & Professional Experience

Research, applied AI, and data-systems experience.

Graduate Research Intern

SRH University of Applied Sciences, Berlin

Oct 2024 – Mar 2025

  • Research focus: Enabling AI to Understand and Process Abstract Concepts (Supervisor: Prof. Dr. Alexander Iliev).
  • Investigated structured representations and symbolic reasoning for abstract-concept processing.
  • Designed controlled benchmarks, holdout splits, and error taxonomies to distinguish structural learning from surface-level pattern matching.
  • Developed evaluation methodology to assess whether model behaviour reflects generalisable structure or dataset shortcuts.

Master's Thesis Research: CMARL for Autonomous Driving

SRH University of Applied Sciences, Berlin

2024 – 2025

  • Designed a Coordinated Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning framework for autonomous vehicles in mixed-traffic environments using CARLA and PyTorch.
  • Investigated MADDPG-style coordination with GNN/GAT-enhanced communication and safety-rule evaluation.
  • Built training, evaluation, and zero-shot generalisation pipelines with controlled traffic scenarios.
  • Defended thesis with grade 1.0/4.0 (S-tier).

Data Engineering Intern

Ford Werke GmbH

Sept 2024 – Mar 2025

  • Built GCP-based ETL workflows and cloud-native pipelines in BigQuery for CRM and marketing data systems.
  • Designed analyst-facing dashboards in Looker Studio for sales and marketing analytics.
  • Collaborated with analytics teams on quality checks, schema validation, and reproducible data pipelines.

Data Science Intern

AI Variant

May 2023 – Aug 2023

  • Conducted exploratory data analysis and applied forecasting techniques to crude-oil price modelling.
  • Developed data-visualisation interfaces using Streamlit for analytical reporting.

Graduate Trainee Engineer

Siemens

Aug 2022 – Mar 2023

  • Supported QA and conducted manual testing for industrial automation software (PCS Neo).
  • Wrote structured defect logs and contributed to traceability and quality documentation.

Resource Person / Guest Speaker

Bangalore Institute of Technology, Bengaluru, India

2026

  • Delivered invited sessions for engineering students on Generative AI and Prompt Engineering.
  • Contributed practical academic outreach on LLM applications, prompting workflows, and responsible use of modern AI tools.
  • Received certificate of recognition for academic contribution.
